messages.content: revise: My passion for digital adoption research took shape during my PhD. studies, where I pursued a Ph.D. in Management specializing in IT Management. My specific focus was on IT-business strategic alignment, a crucial aspect of digital transformation.

During my doctoral studies, my passion for researching digital adoption blossomed as I embarked on my Ph.D. in Management, specializing in IT Management. Throughout this journey, I had the opportunity to delve deeply into the realm of IT-business strategic alignment, which I discovered to be a vital component of successful digital transformation initiatives.

My dedication to studying digital adoption research grew during my pursuit of a Ph.D. in Management, with a specialization in IT Management. Throughout this program, I honed in on the critical area of IT-business strategic alignment, which plays a pivotal role in successful digital transformation endeavors.

I developed a strong passion for digital adoption research during my doctoral studies in Management, where I specialized in IT Management. Throughout my program, I concentrated my efforts on examining the significant role of IT-business strategic alignment as a fundamental aspect of driving successful digital transformation.

revise: My passion for digital adoption research took shape during my PhD. in Management, specializing in IT Management. My specific focus was on IT-business strategic alignment, a crucial aspect of digital transformation.

During my pursuit of a Ph.D. in Management, with a specialization in IT Management, my passion for digital adoption research began to take form. More specifically, I dedicated my studies to exploring the crucial role of IT-business strategic alignment in the context of digital transformation.

edit: My passion for digital adoption research blossomed during my PhD studies, where I pursued a Ph.D. in Management, specializing in IT Management. My specific focus was on IT-business strategic alignment, recognizing its importance as a vital component of digital transformation.

My passion for digital adoption research flourished during my doctoral studies, where I pursued a Ph.D. in Management specializing in IT Management. I focused my research on the crucial aspect of IT-business strategic alignment, recognizing its significance as a vital component of successful digital transformation initiatives.

sumarize: In my search for a prominent professor in digital health research in early 2022, I came across Prof. Dr. Till Winkler, who holds the prestigious Chair of Information Management at the University of Hagen in Germany. With a strong publication record in reputable journals such as the Journal of Management Information Systems, Journal of the Association of Information Systems, MIS Quarterly Executive, and Health Policy & Technology, Prof. Dr. Winkler is well-established in the field. He also serves in editorial roles for reputable journals and has been the recipient of the Early Career Award from the Association of Information Systems. Additionally, Prof. Dr. Winkler has proven expertise in successfully acquiring and managing third-party funding for research projects. Therefore, I contacted Dr. Till Winkler to express my interest in a digital health research opportunity and he thoroughly assessed my qualifications. After conducting an interview and carefully considering my expertise and skills, Dr. Winkler determined that I am well-suited to conduct a research project in this field. Encouraged by his evaluation, I prepared and submitted a research proposal titled “The Influence of Long-Term Orientation on Patient Attitudes Towards Digital Health: A Cross-Country Study in Germany and Iran.” Fortunately, my proposal was approved and I was awarded a fund of 13,104 Euro. Consequently, I commenced my funded research project at the University of Hagen in July 2022.

In early 2022, while searching for a prominent professor in digital health research, I discovered Prof. Dr. Till Winkler, who holds the esteemed Chair of Information Management at the University of Hagen in Germany. Prof. Dr. Winkler possesses a strong publication record in renowned journals, including the Journal of Management Information Systems, Journal of the Association of Information Systems, MIS Quarterly Executive, and Health Policy & Technology. He has also held editorial roles for respected journals and received the Early Career Award from the Association of Information Systems. Dr. Winkler is well-versed in acquiring and managing third-party funding for research projects. I reached out to him to express my interest in a digital health research opportunity, and after an interview and careful evaluation of my qualifications, he determined that I was well-suited to conduct a research project in this field. Encouraged by his assessment, I submitted a research proposal titled “The Influence of Long-Term Orientation on Patient Attitudes Towards Digital Health: A Cross-Country Study in Germany and Iran,” which was approved, and I received a funding of 13,104 Euro. Consequently, my funded research project commenced at the University of Hagen in July 2022.

revise: Given the importance of my project, I have had the opportunity to expand it further, leading to five research papers, where I am working on them and are anticipated to be ongoing until August 2024. Recognizing the value of continuing these research endeavors for the University of Hagen, and with their satisfaction regarding my contributions, my stay at the university has been extended until the end of August 2024.

Due to the significance of my project, I have had the chance to broaden its scope, resulting in the development of five research papers. Currently, I am actively engaged in working on these papers, with the expectation that they will continue to evolve until August 2024. Acknowledging the value of my ongoing research endeavors for the University of Hagen, and with their satisfaction concerning my contributions, my stay at the university has been extended until the conclusion of August 2024.

edit: After staying at Hagen University for about 5-6 months, while my visa was extended until the end of August 2023, I returned to Iran to continue my research in Iran, considering that part of my research was a cross country study between Germany and Iran. .

After spending approximately 5-6 months at Hagen University, my visa was extended until the end of August 2023. At that point, I made the decision to return to Iran to continue my research, as a significant part of my project involved conducting a cross-country study between Germany and Iran.

revise: After staying at Hagen University for about 5-6 months, while my visa was extended until the end of August 2023, I returned to Iran to continue my research in Iran, considering that part of my research was an international research between Germany and Iran. .

Following a period of around 5-6 months at Hagen University, during which time my visa was extended until the end of August 2023, I made the decision to return to Iran in order to continue my research. This choice was made considering the international nature of my project, which involves research conducted between Germany and Iran.
